claim: LLMs have been trained on decades of Unix/Linux file system semantics and commands, making file systems a highly intuitive and native interface for agent interactions compared to custom APIs
actionable: Leverage file system operations (read, write, grep, find) as your primary interface for agent tools; this requires minimal additional training versus custom API paradigms.
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Carter Rabasa: File Systems Are the New Primitive for AI Agents

claim: File systems satisfy four core requirements for agent long-term memory: retaining facts across sessions, selective fact retrieval, updatability over time, and human inspectability
actionable: When designing agent memory systems, evaluate solutions against these four criteria; file systems naturally meet all of them without additional abstraction layers.
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Carter Rabasa: File Systems Are the New Primitive for AI Agents

claim: Unix file descriptors unify heterogeneous resources (files, processes, IO devices) under a single abstraction, allowing consistent manipulation via identical commands—a principle LLMs understand implicitly
actionable: Design your agent tool interfaces to follow Unix philosophy: expose diverse backend systems (databases, APIs, services) through a unified file-like interface when possible.
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Carter Rabasa: File Systems Are the New Primitive for AI Agents

claim: OpenAI's agent SDK supports remote file system mounts (including Box, Chroma, and others), enabling agents to operate on cloud-based file systems as if they were local—no API training required
actionable: Use file system mounts in OpenAI agent SDK or similar frameworks to give agents access to cloud storage; this reduces context window overhead and leverages native LLM file skills.
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Carter Rabasa: File Systems Are the New Primitive for AI Agents

claim: Human-in-the-loop agent workflows benefit from file system interfaces because humans are trained on file systems; a shared file-based interface requires zero training for human operators
actionable: For hybrid human-agent workflows, use a shared file system (local or cloud) as the collaboration interface rather than building specialized dashboards or APIs.
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Carter Rabasa: File Systems Are the New Primitive for AI Agents

claim: File systems provide audit trails and inspectability natively; cloud file systems (Box, S3) add versioning and governance controls, making them ideal for production agents requiring compliance
actionable: Deploy agents with cloud-based file system backends (Box, S3) rather than in-memory storage to enable audit logging, versioning, and compliance reporting.
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Carter Rabasa: File Systems Are the New Primitive for AI Agents

claim: Rather than building bespoke database schemas and APIs for agents, create shims that expose existing systems (databases, vector stores) through file system semantics—LLMs handle this translation invisibly
actionable: Build file system wrapper layers (FUSE mounts, custom shims) over your databases and services instead of exposing raw API schemas to agents.
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Carter Rabasa: File Systems Are the New Primitive for AI Agents

claim: The principle of riding model priors means leveraging interfaces and paradigms the LLM is already deeply trained on rather than inventing new ones; Unix file systems exemplify this principle perfectly
actionable: When designing agent interfaces, research what the LLM is already trained on (Unix commands, file operations, standard protocols) and leverage those rather than training on custom abstractions.
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Carter Rabasa: File Systems Are the New Primitive for AI Agents
